pathTraversalFixes.issue: record.targetFile (ultimately client-supplied via POST /odi/heal request body) was passed unsanitized into path.join(process.cwd(), targetFile), allowing a crafted targetFile like '../../../etc/hosts' to read/write arbitrary files outside the project root.
pathTraversalFixes.fix: Added _safeResolve() helper that resolves against ROOT and rejects any result that escapes it; applied at all 3 call sites.
pathTraversalFixes.verifiedLive: Direct call with a traversal payload ('../../../../../../etc/hosts') returns {ok:false, error:'targetFile escapes project root...'}; a legitimate in-tree path (frontend/src/App.jsx) passes the guard and proceeds normally.

pathTraversalFixes.issue: Identical unsanitized path.resolve(ROOT, targetFile) pattern, reachable from the same POST /odi/heal-adjacent ODI-9 patch routes.
pathTraversalFixes.fix: Same _safeResolve() pattern added and applied at all 3 call sites.
pathTraversalFixes.verifiedLive: generatePatch() called directly with a traversal payload returns the expected rejection; a real in-tree path proceeds normally.

connectorFixes.issue: Razorpay health-probe hit /v1/payment-links (hyphen) — wrong endpoint, real Razorpay API path is /v1/payment_links (underscore).
connectorFixes.verifiedLive: With real rzp_live credentials in .env, the connector flipped from PARTIAL(404) to CONNECTED — 'Razorpay API authenticated, webhook secret set'.

duplicateEnginesNotMerged.decision: NOT merged. Each has real mounted routes and real (if self-referential) live data flowing through them. Merging 14 engines into a shared implementation is itself an architecture redesign, forbidden by this mission's explicit rules. Reported, not touched.

simulatedButUsedByUI.subsystem: Physical/device orchestration family (automationScenarioEngine.cjs, deviceHealthEngine.cjs, deviceOrchestrationEngine.cjs, experimentOrchestratorEngine.cjs, infrastructureRecoveryEngine.cjs, physicalWorkflowEngine.cjs)
simulatedButUsedByUI.reason: When no real physical/IoT device is connected (true in any environment without real hardware attached), these engines transparently fall back to a response that explicitly self-labels as mock (e.g. {ok:true, mock:true, ...} or result:'mock_ok') rather than fabricating a fake-but-unlabeled success. This is honest degraded-mode behavior for a feature category with no real backing infrastructure available anywhere in this repo, not a hidden fake presented as real.
simulatedButUsedByUI.actionTaken: None — removing would either break the endpoint's only response path or require building real device connectivity (new architecture + real hardware, both out of scope). Reported precisely per mission Phase 3 instruction: 'If simulation but used by UI: report exactly why.'

priorFixesReconfirmedLive: 5 no-op-in-file-guard corrections (approvalRoutes.js, computerController.js, founderTwin.js, workforceOS.js, companyFactory.js — an in-file requireAuth resolved to a no-op due to a bad require path; the real enforcement is the index.js prefix mount)
organizations.js + workspace.js RBAC (req.body id-forwarding, not req.query)
apiRoutes.groundTruthCorrection: Rate-limiter middleware is used by 7 files, not 8 as a partial prior scan suggested — commercial.js's 'rateLimited' is a data field in a status response, not a require() of the middleware. Real users: accounts.js, auth.js, browser.js, jarvis.js, odi.js, runtime.js, whatsapp.js.
apiRoutes.liveAuthorizationProof: A second 'attacker' identity received 403 on both DELETE and GET of another user's org (requires permission: delete_org / Not a member of this organization) — confirms real authz + tenant isolation, not just authentication.
apiRoutes.mostImportantRemainingGap: Rate-limiting coverage is thin: only 7 of 126 route files apply the limiter. Every org-ladder, POST-Omega domain, coding, and production-tooling route is unthrottled. All are auth-gated (not an open door), but a single valid operator token could issue unbounded requests to expensive endpoints (/computer/*, /research/*, AI-backed /coding/*). Hardening gap, not a correctness/auth bug. Not fixed this pass — applying rateLimiter(...) per-prefix across ~119 additional route families is a larger, more invasive change than the mission's per-bug-fix scope.

notAutonomous.list: The ~14 duplicate *Org engines have zero self-trigger (0 setInterval/cron); they only run on an API call or an AgentSupervisor persona tick, and 100% of their ticked actions are self-contained JSON mutations with no real external connector call or repo/infra modification.

trueSelfCorrection.evidence: backend/services/autonomousDecisionEngine.cjs is a stateless deterministic rule-matcher with static per-rule confidence constants (0.7-1.0, hardcoded, never adjusted). agents/runtime/executionChainPlanner.cjs is a static regex-to-template matcher with no revision logic. backend/services/executionRecovery.cjs selectStrategy() is the closest analog but is an attempt-count-keyed escalation tree (same failure -> different strategy as attempts climb), not confidence-based revision of a prior judgment. Live-observed: a fresh 65s idle boot showed the self-heal probe healing 5 real failed cycles, ALL choosing the identical strategy (retry_with_backoff) at the identical confidence (14%, static) and re-dispatching the identical work. Confidence is recorded and logged but never changes runtime behavior. Grep of live logs for revision/reconsideration language: zero matches.
trueSelfCorrection.conclusion: The system demonstrates real Recovery (retry the same failed thing) and real static Escalation (different strategy keyed on attempt count), but never demonstrates recognizing that a prior autonomous DECISION's judgment was wrong and choosing a different approach because of that judgment. This capability is NOT IMPLEMENTED, not partially implemented.

autonomousRuntime.liveEvidence: 70s+ idle-window observation with zero API calls from the auditor showed the log growing from ~520 to 5000+ lines: real execSync(git status)/exec(pm2 jlist) firing, 80 autonomous dispatch batches making 1585 real outbound HTTP calls to AI providers, and a self-heal probe detecting and re-executing 5 real failed cycles — all with zero human trigger.

fixedThisSession.issue: Generated release-notes wget URL used ooplix-server-v${{ github.ref_name }}.tar.gz — since github.ref_name already includes the leading 'v' (e.g. v1.0.0-rc6), this produced a doubled-v filename (ooplix-server-vv1.0.0-rc6.tar.gz) that does not match the real uploaded asset name (ooplix-server-v1.0.0-rc6.tar.gz, built from VERSION with the v already stripped).
fixedThisSession.fix: Removed the redundant hardcoded 'v', now reads ooplix-server-${{ github.ref_name }}.tar.gz.
fixedThisSession.verified: Manual substitution check confirms the corrected string matches the real PACKAGE_NAME construction at release.yml:72.
